next_action: Build Poisson/Skellam goal model for MLS+EPL as a separate margin model. Baseline analysis (2026-04-14, CI-gated) showed MLS and EPL margin models cannot be distinguished from predict-zero at 95% CI (MLS: MAE-nv0 CI [-0.046, +0.035]; EPL: [-0.039, +0.093]). Winner/Brier model works fine — problem is specific to the margin formulation applied to a ternary outcome. Math + Domain both CLEAR. Target: margin-MAE-minus-nv0 CI entirely below zero on both MLS and EPL test slice. Defer MLB/NHL tweaks — they already beat predict-zero but by trivially small effect sizes.

open_prs.title: Session handoff (post-merge): refresh docs + capture handoff-discipline lesson
open_prs.status: this PR — merge to land the handoff doc itself

models.v1: v0 + flip when visitor has 10+ more wins
models.v2: v1 + point differential (4 discrete buckets, superseded by v5)
models.v4-spread: continuous margin model + INJURY adjustment + MLB pitcher ERA
models.v5: continuous sigmoid + INJURY adjustment — prob = sigmoid(scale * (homeDiff - awayDiff + homeAdv + injuryAdj))

backtesting_status.v4_spread_margin_mae_baseline: DONE 2026-04-14 — data/baselines/baseline-2026-04-14.json with bootstrap 95% CIs. 16,777 games across 6 sports, closes debt #13. Verdict (paired MAE vs predict-zero, 95% CI): NBA/NFL/MLB/NHL ✓ beat predict-zero. MLS/EPL ~ tie predict-zero on full slice — margin model cannot be distinguished from a constant for soccer. EPL test slice (N=140) shows ✗ LOSES but small N.
backtesting_status.v4_spread_ats_backtest: CANNOT DO — no historical odds data
backtesting_status.injury_adjusted_vs_naive_historical: CANNOT DO — no injury data before 2026-04-13

per_sport_baseline_findings_2026_04_14.methodology: 1000-sample bootstrap, paired diffs within resample, CI at 2.5/97.5 percentiles. In-sample w.r.t. parameter calibration. See learnings.md section 'per-sport-baseline-debt13' for full context.

nba.strategy: parameters at local optimum; gains require new features (rest/travel/back-to-backs)

nfl.strategy: parameters at local optimum; gains require new features (positional injury, rest)

mlb.strategy: statistically significant but effect is trivial (d≈0.01); structural gains likely require bullpen + rest, not parameter tweaks

nhl.strategy: starting goalie is the most-cited missing signal; defer to after soccer Poisson

mls.strategy: margin model indistinguishable from predict-zero — soccer Poisson/Skellam is the priority

epl.strategy: same as MLS; winner model works but margin model provides no value — Poisson first

prs_open_note: PR #26 is this very session's handoff doc. After it merges, prs_open returns to 0 and the next session starts clean.
